subject: ela
description: Print all upper- and lowercase letters.

subject: ela
description: Write narratives that retell two or more appropriately sequenced events, including relevant details and a sense of closure.

subject: ela
description: Write opinions about a topic or text with at least one supporting reason from a source and a sense of closure.

subject: ela
description: Write expository texts about a topic, using a source, providing facts and a sense of closure.

subject: ela
description: With guidance and support from adults, improve writing, as needed, by planning, revising, and editing.

subject: ela
description: Present information orally using complete sentences and appropriate volume.

subject: ela
description: Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate to grade level.

subject: ela
description: Participate in research to gather information to answer a question about a single topic.

subject: ela
description: Use a multimedia element to enhance oral or written tasks.

subject: ela
description: Identify and use digital tools to produce and publish writing individually or with peers and with support from adults.

subject: ela
description: Locate the title, table of contents, names of author(s) and illustrator(s), and glossary of books.

subject: ela
description: Demonstrate phonological awareness. a. Segment spoken words into initial, medial, and final phonemes, including words with digraphs, blends, and trigraphs. b. Orally blend initial, medial, and final phonemes together to produce a single-syllable word that includes digraphs, blends, or trigraphs. c. Blend single-syllable spoken words with at least five phonemes. d. Segment single-syllable spoken words with at least five phonemes. e. Segment and blend phonemes in multi-syllable spoken words.

subject: ela
description: Use knowledge of grade-appropriate phonics and word-analysis skills to decode words accurately. a. Decode words using knowledge of spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs, trigraphs, and blends. b. Decode simple words with r-controlled vowels. c. Decode and encode regularly spelled one-syllable words. d. Decode words with inflectional endings. e. Decode two-syllable words with regular patterns by breaking the words into syllables. f. Decode words that use final -e and vowel teams to make long-vowel sounds.

subject: ela
description: Read grade-level texts with accuracy, automaticity, and appropriate prosody or expression. a. Recognize and read with automaticity the grade-level sight words.

subject: ela
description: Identify and describe the main story elements in a story.

subject: ela
description: Identify and explain the moral of a story. This benchmark is not present in kindergarten.

subject: ela
description: Explain who is telling the story using context clues.

subject: ela
description: Identify stanzas and line breaks in poems.

subject: ela
description: Use text features including titles, headings, captions, graphs, maps, glossaries, and/or illustrations to demonstrate understanding of texts.

subject: ela
description: Identify the topic of and relevant details in a text.

subject: ela
description: Explain similarities and differences between information provided in visuals and words in an informational text. This benchmark is not present in kindergarten.

subject: ela
description: Identify an author’s opinion(s) about the topic.

subject: ela
description: Identify and explain descriptive words and phrases in text(s).

subject: ela
description: Retell a text in oral or written form to enhance comprehension: a. Use main story elements at the beginning, middle, and end for a literary text; b. Use topic and important details for an informational text.

subject: ela
description: Compare and contrast two texts on the same topic.

subject: ela
description: Use grade-level academic vocabulary appropriately in speaking and writing.

subject: ela
description: Identify and use frequently occurring base words and their common inflections in grade-level content.

subject: ela
description: Identify and use picture clues, context clues, word relationships, reference materials, and/or background knowledge to determine the meaning of unknown words.

subject: ela
description: Write narratives using an appropriate pace to create tension, mood, and/or tone.

subject: ela
description: Write to argue a position, supporting claims using logical reasoning and credible evidence from multiple sources, rebutting counterclaims with relevant evidence, using a logical organizational structure, elaboration, purposeful transitions, and maintaining a formal and objective tone.

subject: ela
description: Write expository texts to explain and analyze information from multiple sources, using a logical organization, purposeful transitions, and a tone and voice appropriate to the task.

subject: ela
description: Improve writing by considering feedback from adults, peers, and/or online editing tools, revising to address the needs of a specific audience.

subject: ela
description: Present information orally, with a logical organization and coherent focus, with credible evidence, creating a clear perspective.

subject: ela
description: Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate to grade level.

subject: ela
description: Conduct research to answer a question, refining the scope of the question to align with findings and synthesizing information from multiple reliable and valid sources.

subject: ela
description: Create digital presentations to improve understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence.

subject: ela
description: Use online collaborative platforms to create and export publication-ready quality writing tailored to a specific audience, integrating multimedia elements.

subject: ela
description: Analyze how key elements enhance or add layers of meaning and/or style in a literary text.

subject: ela
description: Analyze and compare universal themes and their development throughout a literary text.

subject: ela
description: Analyze coming of age experiences reflected in a text and how the author represents conflicting perspectives.

subject: ela
description: Analyze how authors create multiple layers of meaning and/or ambiguity in a poem.

subject: ela
description: Analyze the impact of multiple text structures and the use of features in text(s).

subject: ela
description: Analyze the central idea(s) of historical American speeches and essays.

subject: ela
description: Analyze an author’s choices in establishing and achieving purpose(s) in historical American speeches and essays.

subject: ela
description: Compare the development of two opposing arguments on the same topic, evaluating the effectiveness and validity of the claims and analyzing the ways in which the authors use the same information to achieve different ends.

subject: ela
description: Analyze how figurative language creates mood in text(s).

subject: ela
description: Paraphrase content from grade-level texts.

subject: ela
description: Analyze how mythical, classical, or religious texts have been adapted.

subject: ela
description: Analyze an author’s use of rhetoric in a text.

subject: ela
description: Integrate academic vocabulary appropriate to grade level in speaking and writing.

subject: ela
description: Apply knowledge of etymology and derivations to determine meanings of words and phrases in grade-level content.

subject: ela
description: Apply knowledge of context clues, figurative language, word relationships, reference materials, and/or background knowledge to determine the connotative and denotative meaning of words and phrases, appropriate to grade level.

subject: ela
description: Write complex narratives using appropriate techniques to establish multiple perspectives.

subject: ela
description: Write literary analyses to support claims, using logical reasoning, credible evidence from sources, and elaboration, demonstrating an understanding of literary elements.

subject: ela
description: Write an analysis of complex texts using logical organization and a tone and voice appropriate to the task and audience, demonstrating an understanding of the subject.

subject: ela
description: Improve writing by considering feedback from adults, peers, and/or online editing tools, revising to improve clarity, structure, and style.

subject: ela
description: Present information orally, with a logical organization, coherent focus, and credible evidence, while employing effective rhetorical devices where appropriate.

subject: ela
description: Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate to grade level.

subject: ela
description: Conduct literary research to answer a question, refining the scope of the question to align with interpretations of texts and synthesizing information from primary and secondary sources.

subject: ela
description: Create digital presentations to improve the experience of the audience.

subject: ela
description: Create and export quality writing tailored to a specific audience, integrating multimedia elements, publishing to an online or LAN site.

subject: ela
description: Evaluate how key elements enhance or add layers of meaning and/or style in a literary text.

subject: ela
description: Track and analyze universal themes in literary texts from different times and places.

subject: ela
description: Analyze the author’s choices in using juxtaposition to define character perspective.

subject: ela
description: Analyze ways in which poetry reflects themes and issues of its time period.

description: Evaluate the structure(s) and features in texts.

subject: ela
description: Analyze the central idea(s) of speeches and essays from the Classical Period.

subject: ela
description: Analyze an author’s choices in establishing and achieving purpose(s) in speeches and essays from the Classical Period.

subject: ela
description: Compare the development of multiple arguments on the same topic, evaluating the effectiveness and validity of the claims, the authors’ reasoning, and the ways in which the authors use the same information to achieve different ends.

subject: ela
description: Analyze the author’s use of figurative language and explain examples of allegory.

description: Paraphrase content from grade-level texts.

description: Compare and contrast how contemporaneous authors address related topics, comparing the authors’ use of reasoning and analyzing the texts within the context of the time period.

description: Evaluate an author’s use of rhetoric in text.

description: Integrate academic vocabulary appropriate to grade level in speaking and writing.

subject: ela
description: Apply knowledge of etymology and derivations to determine meanings of words and phrases in grade-level content.

subject: ela
description: Apply knowledge of context clues, figurative language, word relationships, reference materials, and/or background knowledge to determine the connotative and denotative meaning of words and phrases, appropriate to grade level.

description: Write complex narratives using appropriate techniques to establish multiple perspectives and convey universal themes.

subject: ela
description: Write arguments to support claims based on an in-depth analysis of topics or texts, using valid reasoning and credible evidence from sources, elaboration, and demonstrating a thorough understanding of the subject.

subject: ela
description: Write an in-depth analysis of complex texts using logical organization and appropriate tone and voice, demonstrating a thorough understanding of the subject.

subject: ela
description: Improve writing by considering feedback from adults, peers, and/or online editing tools, revising to enhance purpose, clarity, structure, and style.

subject: ela
description: Present information orally, with a logical organization, coherent focus, and credible evidence, while employing effective rhetorical devices where appropriate.

description: Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate to grade level.

description: Conduct research on a topical issue to answer a question and synthesize information from a variety of sources.

description: Design and evaluate digital presentations for effectiveness.

description: Create, publish, and share multimedia texts through a variety of digital formats.

description: Evaluate the development of character perspective, including conflicting perspectives.

description: Evaluate works of major poets in their historical context.

description: Evaluate the structure(s) and features in text(s), identifying how the author could make the text(s) more effective.

description: Evaluate how an author develops the central idea(s), identifying how the author could make the support more effective.

description: Evaluate an author’s choices in establishing and achieving purpose(s).

description: Compare the development of multiple arguments in related texts, evaluating the validity of the claims, the authors’ reasoning, use of the same information, and/or the authors’ rhetoric.

description: Write personal or fictional narratives using a logical sequence of events, transitions, and an ending.

description: Write opinions about a topic or text with reasons supported by details from a source, use transitions, and provide a conclusion.

description: Write expository texts about a topic, using a source, providing an introduction, facts, transitions, and a conclusion.

description: Improve writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing with guidance and support from adults and feedback from peers.

description: Present information orally using complete sentences, appropriate volume, and clear pronunciation.

description: Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate to grade level.

subject: ela
description: Use knowledge of grade-appropriate phonics and word-analysis skills to decode words. a. Decode words with variable vowel teams (e.g., oo, ea, ou) and vowel diphthongs (e.g., oi, oy, ow). b. Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long and short vowels. c. Decode words with open (e.g., hi, baby, moment) and closed (e.g., bag, sunshine, chop) syllables and consonant -le (e.g., purple, circle, stumble). d. Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes. e. Decode words with silent letter combinations (e.g., knight, comb, island, ghost).

description: Write to argue a position, supporting at least one claim and rebutting at least one counterclaim with logical reasoning, credible evidence from multiple sources, elaboration, and using a logical organizational structure.

subject: ela
description: Write expository texts to explain and analyze information from multiple sources, using relevant supporting details, logical organization, and varied purposeful transitions.

subject: ela
description: Improve writing by planning, editing, considering feedback from adults and peers, and revising for clarity and cohesiveness.

subject: ela
description: Present information orally, in a logical sequence, supporting the central idea with credible evidence.

subject: ela
description: Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate to grade level.

subject: ela
description: Conduct research to answer a question, drawing on multiple reliable and valid sources and generating additional questions for further research.

subject: ela
description: Integrate diverse digital media to emphasize the relevance of a topic or idea in oral or written tasks.

subject: ela
description: Use a variety of digital tools to collaborate with others to produce writing.

subject: ela
description: Analyze the interaction between character development, setting, and plot in a literary text.

subject: ela
description: Analyze two or more themes and their development throughout a literary text.

subject: ela
description: Analyze how an author develops and individualizes the perspectives of different characters.

subject: ela
description: Analyze structure, sound, imagery, and figurative language in poetry.

subject: ela
description: Analyze how individual text sections and/or features convey a purpose and/or meaning in texts.

subject: ela
description: Analyze two or more central ideas and their development throughout a text.

subject: ela
description: Explain how an author establishes and achieves purpose(s) through rhetorical appeals and/or figurative language.

subject: ela
description: Track the development of an argument, analyzing the types of reasoning used and their effectiveness and identifying ways in which the argument could be improved.

subject: ela
description: Analyze how figurative language contributes to meaning and explain examples of symbolism in text(s).

subject: ela
description: Paraphrase content from grade-level texts.

subject: ela
description: Compare and contrast the use or discussion of archetypes in texts.

subject: ela
description: Explain how an author uses rhetorical devices to support or advance an appeal.

subject: ela
description: Integrate academic vocabulary appropriate to grade level in speaking and writing.

subject: ela
description: Apply knowledge of Greek and Latin roots and affixes to determine meanings of words and phrases in grade-level content.

subject: ela
description: Apply knowledge of context clues, figurative language, word relationships, reference materials, and/or background knowledge to determine the connotative and denotative meaning of words and phrases, appropriate to grade level.

subject: ela
description: Write narratives using narrative techniques, varied transitions, and a clearly established point of view.

subject: ela
description: Write to argue a position, supporting claims using logical reasoning and credible evidence from multiple sources, rebutting counterclaims with relevant evidence, using a logical organizational structure, elaboration, purposeful transitions, and a tone appropriate to the task.

subject: ela
description: Write expository texts to explain and analyze information from multiple sources, using a logical organization, varied purposeful transitions, and a tone appropriate to the task.

subject: ela
description: Improve writing by considering feedback from adults, peers, and/or online editing tools, revising for clarity and cohesiveness.

subject: ela
description: Present information orally, with a logical organization and coherent focus, with credible evidence, creating a clear perspective.

subject: ela
description: Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate to grade level.

subject: ela
description: Conduct research to answer a question, drawing on multiple reliable and valid sources and refining the scope of the question to align with findings.

subject: ela
description: Create digital presentations with coherent ideas and a clear perspective.

subject: ela
description: Use online collaborative platforms to create and export publication-ready quality writing tailored to a specific audience.

subject: ela
description: Explain how key elements enhance or add layers of meaning and/or style in a literary text.

subject: ela
description: Analyze universal themes and their development throughout a literary text.

subject: ela
description: Analyze the influence of narrator perspective on a text, explaining how the author creates irony or satire.

description: Analyze the characters, structures, and themes of epic poetry.

subject: ela
description: Analyze how multiple text structures and/or features convey a purpose and/or meaning in texts.

description: Analyze how an author establishes and achieves purpose(s) through rhetorical appeals and/or figurative language.

description: Compare the development of two opposing arguments on the same topic, evaluating the effectiveness and validity of the claims.

description: Explain how figurative language creates mood in text(s).

description: Explain an author’s use of rhetoric in a text.

subject: ela
description: Print many upper- and lowercase letters.

description: Using a combination of drawing, dictating, and/or writing, create narratives with the events in chronological order.

description: With guidance and support from adults, improve drawing and writing, as needed, by planning, revising, and editing.

subject: ela
description: Present information orally using complete sentences.

subject: ela
description: Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate to grade level.

subject: ela
description: Recall information to answer a question about a single topic.

subject: ela
description: Use a multimedia element to enhance oral or written tasks.

subject: ela
description: Demonstrate knowledge of the basic concepts of print. a. Locate a printed word on a page. b. Distinguish letters from words within sentences. Print Concepts c. Match print to speech to demonstrate that language is represented by print. d. Identify parts of a book (front cover, back cover, title page). e. Move top to bottom and left to right on the printed page, returning to the beginning of the next line. f. Identify all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet. g. Recognize that print conveys specific meaning and pictures may support meaning.

subject: ela
description: Demonstrate phonological awareness. a. Blend and segment syllables in spoken words. b. Identify and produce alliterative and rhyming words. c. Blend and segment onset and rimes of single-syllable words. d. Identify the initial, medial, and final sound of spoken words. e. Add or delete phonemes at the beginning or end of a spoken word and say the resulting Phonological Awareness word. f. Segment and blend phonemes in single-syllable spoken words.

subject: ela
description: Use knowledge of grade-appropriate phonics and word-analysis skills to decode words accurately. a. Demonstrate knowledge of the most frequent sound for each consonant. b. Demonstrate knowledge of the short and long sounds for the five major vowels. c. Decode consonant-vowel-consonant (CVC) words. d. Encode consonant-vowel-consonant (CVC) words.

subject: ela
description: Recognize and read with automaticity grade-level high frequency words.

subject: ela
description: Describe the main character(s), setting, and important events in a story.

subject: ela
description: Explain the roles of author and illustrator of a story.

subject: ela
description: Identify rhyme in a poem.

subject: ela
description: Use titles, headings, and illustrations to predict and confirm the topic of texts.

subject: ela
description: Identify the topic of and multiple details in a text.

subject: ela
description: Explain the difference between opinions and facts about a topic.

subject: ela
description: Identify and explain descriptive words in text(s).

subject: ela
description: Retell a text orally to enhance comprehension: a. Use main character(s), setting, and important events for a story. b. Use topic and details for an informational text.

subject: ela
description: Compare and contrast characters’ experiences in stories.

subject: ela
description: Use grade-level academic vocabulary appropriately in speaking and writing.

subject: ela
description: Ask and answer questions about unfamiliar words in grade-level content.

subject: ela
description: Identify and sort common words into basic categories, relating vocabulary to background knowledge.
